title: Al Horford Optimistic He'll Decide To Continue Playing Next Season
summary: Golden State Warriors center Al Horford said Monday he will spend the next several weeks deciding whether to exercise his player option worth nearly $6 million for next season while leaving open the possibility of retirement. "I think that, for me, it has to do with not rushing to anything or not let emotions or heat of the moment determine anything," Horford said. "I just think I've done this the last few years, and it's just kind of taken time to step back and have a clear picture, look at the outlook of the team, looking at the future of the team, how things look, and also seeing kind of where I'm at and how I'm feeling." Horford, who turns 40 on June 3, appeared in just 45 games this season due to a series of injuries, averaging 8.3 points and 4.9 rebounds. Despite limited availability, he became a trusted presence in the locker room and a well-regarded teammate. When asked directly whether retirement was under consideration, Horford stopped short of ruling it out while sounding cautiously optimistic about continuing. "Fortunately for me, I'm healthy," he said. "I feel good. I still feel like I can contribute and play at a very high level still. Those are all things that I'm going to look at. Now that everything kind of gets quiet, you know, it's something that I'll reflect on all that." Horford said Golden State's competitive outlook next season will factor heavily into his choice. He noted that his family has settled comfortably in the Bay Area, which could work in the Warriors' favor as they look to retain him.

title: Brandin Podziemski Wants To Remain With Warriors Long-Term Ahead Of Extension Talks
summary: Golden State Warriors guard Brandin Podziemski expressed a strong desire Monday to remain with the franchise that drafted him, saying he hopes to finalize a contract extension before next season's deadline in October. "I want to be here for the long term," Podziemski said during exit interviews. "It's always special having the team that drafted you, so, hopefully, we can get something done." Podziemski said he is confident his agent, Bill Duffy, and the front office can reach an agreement in the coming months. Podziemski completed his third NBA season averaging 13.8 points, 5.1 rebounds and 3.7 assists while appearing in all 82 regular-season games, a mark reached by only 17 other players league-wide. Podziemski acknowledged that watching teammates Moses Moody and Jonathan Kuminga navigate their respective extensions has shaped his own thinking. "I think both seeing him and Moses do two different things for me helped," Podziemski said. "Just seeing the goods and bads to both sides. I don't think one is better than the other, but seeing that as a teammate and seeing how they handled both situations, I think is going to help me." Moody signed a three-year, $39 million extension in October 2024. Kuminga's negotiations stretched into training camp before he agreed to a two-year, $48.5 million deal, ultimately being dealt to the Atlanta Hawks in February for Kristaps Porzingis.

title: Victor Wembanyama Unanimously Named NBA Defensive Player Of The Year
summary: Victor Wembanyama has unanimously been named the 2025-26 NBA Defensive Player of the Year, becoming the youngest player in league history to win the award at the age of 22. Wembanyama was on his way to winning the award last season before he was ruled out for the season in February due to deep vein thrombosis. Wembanyama is the first player in NBA history to receive all first place votes for Defensive Player of the Year. Chet Holmgren finished second with 76 second place votes and 11 third place votes ahead of Ausar Thompson with nine second place votes and 33 third place votes. Rudy Gobert and Scottie Barnes rounded out the top-5. Derrick White, Cason Wallace, Amen Thompson, Dyson Daniels, OG Anunoby, Jalen Duren, Draymond Green and Bam Adebayo also received Defensive Player of the Year votes. Wembanyama led the NBA in blocks for the third straight season at 3.1 per game and 197 for the season. The San Antonio Spurs allowed just 103.6 points per 100 possessions with Wembanyama on the floor compared to 113.7 with him off. Wembanyama is also a top-3 finisher for Most Valuable Player. Wembanyama is the fourth member of the Spurs to be named Defensive Player of the Year, joining Alvin Robertson, David Robinson and Kawhi Leonard (2X). Despite his extended period of defensive excellence, Tim Duncan was never named Defensive Player of the Year.

title: Joel Embiid Starts Recovery Program After Appendectomy, Timeline Uncertain
summary: Philadelphia 76ers center Joel Embiid has begun a strength and conditioning program following his emergency appendectomy nearly two weeks ago, the team announced Monday. However, he remains in Philadelphia and is effectively ruled out for Tuesday's Game 2 against the Boston Celtics. The 76ers dropped Game 1 on Sunday, 123-91, a result that underscored how dependent Philadelphia's offense is on Embiid's presence. The team hopes he can return at some point during the series, though that window may narrow quickly depending on how competitive the Sixers prove to be. Without Embiid, Boston was able to direct greater defensive focus toward Tyrese Maxey and Paul George, neutralizing Philadelphia's halfcourt attack. The Celtics have no reliable matchup for Embiid in isolation, and his absence allowed Boston to apply suffocating pressure elsewhere. Philadelphia responded with a pointed practice and film session Monday. Nick Nurse indicated the session was more targeted than usual. "Normally, we go over film in general on offense and defense and special teams," Nurse said. "Not today. We were much more specific today." The Sixers were visibly disappointed with both their effort and output in Game 1 and expect a sharper performance going forward despite entering the series as underdogs. Embiid appeared in 38 regular season games, averaging just over 26 points per game. Coming off nearly two years of knee problems, he demonstrated he remains among the league's most imposing offensive forces when healthy. The original plan was to manage his workload during the regular season to ensure he was fully available for the playoffs.

title: Taylor Jenkins Meets With Bucks To Discuss Head Coaching Vacancy
summary: Taylor Jenkins met in Memphis last week with Jon Horst, Wes Edens and Jimmy Haslam to discuss the franchise's head coaching vacancy. The two sides are expected to meet again soon to further discuss the opening. Jenkins was head coach of the Memphis Grizzlies from 2019 until he was abruptly fired near the end of the 2024-25 season. The Grizzlies qualified for the playoffs in four of Jenkins' six seasons as head coach. Jenkins was an assistant coach for the Bucks under Mike Budenholzer during the 2018-19 season before he was hired as head coach of the Grizzlies. Jenkins is considered one of the top coaching free agents of the cycle and could wait to see what ends up happening with other potential jobs such as the Orlando Magi. Doc Rivers stepped down as head coach of the Bucks at the end of the season. The Bucks have yet to signal whether they will trade Giannis Antetokounmpo this offseason, or continue to pursue an extension.

title: Jazz, Hawks Amongst Six NBA Draft Tiebreaker Winners
summary: Six ties among teams with identical regular-season records were broken today through random drawings to determine the order of selection for NBA Draft 2026. The drawings were conducted by NBA President of League Operations Byron Spruell at the NBA office in Secaucus, New Jersey. The tiebreaker process was overseen by Megan DeCesaris, a partner from the accounting firm of Ernst & Young. The results of the drawings: • The Utah Jazz (22-60) won a tiebreaker with the Sacramento Kings. • The New Orleans Pelicans (26-56) won a tiebreaker with the Dallas Mavericks. The Atlanta Hawks own the draft rights to the Pelicans' pick . • The Phoenix Suns (45-37) won a tiebreaker with the Orlando Magic and the Philadelphia 76ers. Second and third place in the tiebreaker drawings went to Philadelphia and Orlando, respectively. • The Toronto Raptors (46-36) won a tiebreaker with the Atlanta Hawks. • The Houston Rockets (52-30) won a tiebreaker with the Cleveland Cavaliers. • The New York Knicks (53-29) won a tiebreaker with the Los Angeles Lakers. Utah is guaranteed to keep their first round pick that is top-eight protected and is now extinguished . NBA Draft Lottery 2026 will be held on Sunday, May 10 and air live on ABC at 3:00 p.m. ET. Pre-Lottery Positions: 1. Washington 2. Indiana 3. Brooklyn 4. Utah 5. Sacramento 6. Memphis 7. New Orleans → Atlanta or Milwaukee 8. Dallas 9. Chicago 10. Milwaukee 11. Golden State 12. LA Clippers → Oklahoma City 13. Miami 14. Charlotte The order for the remainder of the first round picks is as follows: 15. Portland → Chicago 16. Phoenix → Memphis via Orlando 17. Philadelphia → Oklahoma City 18. Orlando → Charlotte via Phoenix 19. Toronto 20. Atlanta → San Antonio 21. Minnesota → Detroit 22. Houston → Philadelphia via Oklahoma City 23. Cleveland → Atlanta 24. New York 25. Los Angeles Lakers 26. Denver 27. Boston 28. Detroit → Minnesota 29. San Antonio → Cleveland via Atlanta 30. Oklahoma City → Dallas via Washington and Philadelphia

title: Draymond Green Says It 'Feels Like' Steve Kerr Has Coached His Last Game With Warriors
summary: Draymond Green publicly predicted Monday that Steve Kerr's 12-year run as Golden State Warriors head coach has come to an end, three days after Kerr himself acknowledged uncertainty about his future with the franchise. "I hope he's our coach next season," Green said on his podcast, "The Draymond Green Show." "You want my opinion? I think not. Just because it feels like that. It felt like that was it." The comments followed an emotional scene on the Phoenix arena floor after the Warriors were eliminated Friday night. Kerr gathered Green and Stephen Curry on the sideline, with microphones capturing him telling the two: "I don't know what's going to happen next, but I love you guys to death. Thank you." Kerr said Friday he plans to spend the next week or two weighing his future. No significant discussions took place over the weekend. Exit interviews with players were held Monday at the team's San Francisco facility, with substantial conversations between Kerr, general manager Mike Dunleavy and controlling owner Joe Lacob anticipated in the coming days. Kerr has placed his own return odds at roughly 50-50, per ESPN. Team sources indicate owner Lacob wants to see genuine enthusiasm from Kerr about continuing - not a reluctant commitment rooted in loyalty to Curry and Green. If Kerr does return, management prefers a multiyear contract rather than a farewell-season arrangement driven by nostalgia. "I still love coaching, but I get it," Kerr said Friday. "These jobs all have an expiration date. There is a run that happens, and when the run ends, sometimes it's time for new blood and new ideas." Green's own future in Golden State remains unsettled. He holds a one-year, $27.6 million player option and is uncertain about his path forward. Curry is entering the final season of his contract, though both sides have expressed mutual interest in an extension. "I've never been so uncertain since earlier in my career in what happens next," Green said. "But I'm truly at a loss now because you don't know what direction will be next. ... I also hope I'm on this team next season. We also don't know that. Man, if it was, what a run."

title: Bulls Identify Six Candidates In President Of Basketball Operations Search
summary: The Chicago Bulls have launched their search for a new head of basketball operations, receiving permission to interview five front office executives from around the league, sources told ESPN. Agent Austin Brown is also expected to be considered for the position. Interviews are scheduled to begin this week. The organization is targeting a hire shortly before or after the mid-May draft combine. The vacancy was created April 6 when the Bulls dismissed executive vice president Arturas Karnisovas and general manager Marc Eversley following six years that produced just one playoff appearance. Bulls CEO Michael Reinsdorf has been direct that a commitment to retaining head coach Billy Donovan is the one requirement for any incoming decision-maker. "If I interview someone and they're not sold on Billy, they're not sold on a Hall of Fame coach; they're not sold on a person who's won championships in college, who's gone deep in the playoffs with Oklahoma City," Reinsdorf said April 7. "... If Billy wants to be our coach and someone's not interested in that, then they're probably not the right candidate for us." The five candidates granted interview permission are Minnesota Timberwolves GM Matt Lloyd, Detroit Pistons senior vice president Dennis Lindsey, Atlanta Hawks senior vice president Bryson Graham, Cleveland Cavaliers GM Mike Gansey and San Antonio Spurs assistant GM Dave Telep. Lloyd brings 17 years of prior Chicago experience, while Lindsey has three decades of front office and scouting work, including seven years as Utah Jazz general manager. Graham rose from intern to GM over 15 seasons with the New Orleans Pelicans. Gansey helped rebuild Cleveland into an Eastern Conference contender, and Telep spent 13 seasons in San Antonio's front office after a career as a prominent recruiting analyst. Brown, a Chicago native and co-head of CAA's basketball division, currently represents prominent players including Donovan Mitchell, Trae Young and Cooper Flagg. He was previously offered the Bulls' GM role during the 2020 search. Three agents, Bob Myers, Rob Pelinka and Leon Rose, have successfully transitioned to top NBA front office positions over the past 15 years.

title: Steve Ballmer Rejected Warriors' Pursuit Of Kawhi Leonard At Trade Deadline
summary: The Golden State Warriors made a serious push to acquire Los Angeles Clippers forward Kawhi Leonard before the February trade deadline, only to receive a firm rejection from Clippers governor Steve Ballmer, league sources told ESPN. The Warriors had contacted the Clippers about Leonard's availability several times over the years, each time getting quickly turned away. This time, however, Los Angeles had signaled a potential shift in direction after trading James Harden and engaging multiple teams in discussions that ultimately produced a deal with the Indiana Pacers for Ivica Zubac. Golden State's offer reportedly included substantial draft capital and multiple valued players. The Clippers brought the proposal to Ballmer, but the answer was no by the morning of the trade deadline. Accounts of how advanced the talks became differ sharply between the two organizations. Warriors personnel believed the Clippers were genuinely weighing the concept. On the Los Angeles side, Ballmer's rejection was described as unwavering. The Leonard pursuit was part of a broader pattern for Golden State. Over the past two seasons, the Warriors also sought LeBron James, Paul George, Lauri Markkanen, Jaren Jackson Jr. and Giannis Antetokounmpo in efforts to build a championship contender around Stephen Curry. The same deadline period also had the Warriors navigate the Jonathan Kuminga standoff, which concluded with Golden State dealing him for Kristaps Porzingis. Sources indicate the Warriors plan to revisit Leonard's availability this offseason, submit a fresh offer for Antetokounmpo and explore the free agent market for LeBron James. Golden State could also retain Porzingis below his current $30.7 million expiring contract or use him as a trade asset in a sign-and-trade for another prominent player. Leonard is on a $50.3 million expiring contract and declined to address his future with the Clippers when they were eliminated from the playoffs last week, including on a potential extension. The Clippers can offer a maximum of $126.1 million across those two seasons.

Green has continued to decline as an offensive player and no longer can make a Defensive Player of the Year type of impact on a nightly basis, but he remains one of the NBA's best talents on that side of the floor as evidenced by his performance in the play-in against Kawhi Leonard. Green, who can be volatile both internally within the team and on the floor, has received praise for his "attitude, approach and leadership through an adverse season." The Warriors included Green in trade talks for Giannis Antetokounmpo, but there has never been a desire to trade him. "I'm not retiring from basketball," Green said. "I still love to play. I still think I'm pretty decent. But this league is interesting. You don't really control everything. For me, I hope I've done enough to be here. If I ain't done enough, I don't want to be here. I don't ever want, 'Yo, we just going to keep him around because of what he's done before.' What can I still do?"

title: Steve Kerr 50-50 On Returning Next Season, Warriors Seek Multi-Year Commitment
summary: The Golden State Warriors will want Steve Kerr to sign a multi-year extension if he decides he wants to continue as head coach next season. Joe Lacob is believed to want to hear from Kerr that he wants to continue the grind of being an NBA head coach rather than merely wanting to see out the end of Stephen Curry's career. Kerr is expected to take one to two weeks to consider his future before meeting with Lacob and Mike Dunleavy Jr. The Warriors do want the coaching situation resolved quickly before turning their attention to roster and strategic issues. Should Kerr decide he wants to remain as head coach, the Warriors want to discuss diversifying the offense and also winning the possession battle more frequently while relying less on three-point variance. If Kerr decides to step away, the front office will cast a wide search for his replacement. The Warriors could even consider a college coach, however, it could be challenging to hire a novice NBA head coach during the final seasons of Curry, Draymond Green and Jimmy Butler. Curry and Green have publicly and polities expressed a preference for Kerr to continue as head coach. "I want Coach to be happy," Curry said. "I want him to be excited for the job. I want him to believe he's the right guy for the job. I want him to have an opportunity to enjoy what he does, whatever that means for him. Everybody's plan is their own. He knows how I feel about him." Kerr has reportedly continued to say to confidants that he enjoys the job, but others close to him "theorize he's burnt-out." Kerr told ESPN this week that he's 50-50 on his decision on whether to continue or step away.

title: Victor Wembanyama On Scoring 35 Points In Playoff Debut: 'The Atmosphere Was Different'
summary: Victor Wembanyama finished with 35 points as the San Antonio Spurs defeated the Portland Trail Blazers 111-98 in Game 1 to open the series on Sunday. Wembanyama set a Spurs record for the most points in a playoff debut, surpassing Tim Duncan's 32 points. "The first time I stepped on the court, even for warmups, I felt the atmosphere was different," Wembanyama said. "Everybody was ready. The fans were ready. It's probably the most excitement I've seen this year in this arena. In terms of tactics, the sport aspect of it, it was different because we had more time to prepare." Wembanyama finished 13-of-21 from the field and 5-of-6 from three-point range. The Blazers also went 0-11 when Wembanyama was the contesting defender. "I thought he was ready," coach Mitch Johnson said. "There's an approach that we all have in terms of expectation of a heightened level of preparation, detail, nuance, competitiveness, physicality, everything. There's a real desire for that young man to participate in that. This was his first playoff game, and he has lofty expectations and goals for himself." Deni Avdija lead the Blazers with 30 points. Wembanyama added that he expects the Blazers to bring more physicality to Game 2. "Of course," Wembanyama said. "It's their identity. It's something to be expected, and it's also to be expected that they'll double up on that in the next games. But we're ready." San Antonio hosts the Blazers again on Tuesday when they will be -12.5 point favorites .

title: Magic Shock Top-Seeded Pistons 112-101 In Game 1
summary: Paolo Banchero posted 23 points, nine rebounds, and four assists Sunday night to lead eighth-seeded Orlando Magic past top-seeded Detroit 112-101 in Game 1, extending the longest home playoff losing streak in NBA history. Detroit has now dropped 11 consecutive postseason games at home, a drought stretching back to 2008. Orlando never trailed, jumping out to an 18-5 lead midway through the first quarter. It marked the first time since seeding began in 1984 that a No. 1 seed failed to lead at any point in its playoff opener, per ESPN Research. Cade Cunningham posted a playoff career-high 39 points for Detroit. Tobias Harris added 17, but the rest of the Pistons were largely quiet offensively. Detroit's 11-point defeat ranks tied for the third-largest margin in a playoff opener by a 60-win team. Franz Wagner scored 11 of his 19 points in the fourth quarter to help close out the win. Desmond Bane and Wendell Carter Jr. each contributed 17 points for Orlando, with Jalen Suggs adding 16. Detroit pulled even at 65-65 in the third quarter on a Cunningham three-pointer before Banchero answered with a pull-up jumper and Bane hit a three to restore a comfortable Orlando cushion. The Magic led 81-74 entering the fourth. "It's a step in the right direction for the team, for this group, with our past track record on the road in the playoffs," Banchero said. "We've just got to get ready for Wednesday, see what we did well (and) what we can clean up. I expect (the Pistons) to come out with a lot of energy and we've just got to be ready for it." Neither franchise has advanced past the first round in at least 15 years. The Pistons are -9.5 point favorites for Game 2 on Wednesday .

title: Thunder Open Championship Defense With 35-Point Blowout Of Suns
summary: The Oklahoma City Thunder launched their title defense with a 119-84 dismantling of the Phoenix Suns in Game 1 Sunday at Paycom Center, matching last postseason's opening blowout of the Memphis Grizzlies by again winning by at least 35 points. The Thunder became just the third team in NBA history to open back-to-back postseasons with wins of that margin, joining the Los Angeles Lakers of 1986 and 1987. Oklahoma City converted 19 Suns turnovers into 34 points, producing a 34-2 advantage in points off turnovers, the largest such differential recorded in a playoff game during the play-by-play era. "Every time you're playing against them, you've got to value the ball, value possessions, got to get a shot up -- and we did not do that," Suns coach Jordan Ott said. "Their physicality, their athleticism, their size, their length [and] their personnel forces you to continuously make good decisions and quick decisions. That's part of it for sure that we'll have to clean up." Jalen Williams paced Oklahoma City with 22 points, seven rebounds, and six assists. Reigning MVP Shai Gilgeous-Alexander contributed 25 points and seven assists despite shooting 5-of-18 and sitting out the fourth quarter. "I'm sure those guys take the regular-season game serious, but they feel like this is the time of year," Devin Booker said. "Every time down, [we can't] turn it over. I think that fuels everything -- fuels them, fuels the crowd. Turns into a domino effect." Phoenix faced a difficult path into Game 1, having secured their playoff spot just Friday night with a play-in victory over the Golden State Warriors before traveling Saturday for a 2:30 p.m. local tipoff against the top overall seed on Sunday. The Thunder led by 15 after one quarter, 21 at halftime, and 31 heading into the fourth. Oklahoma City hosts the Suns again on Wednesday in Game 2 when they will be -17.5 point favorites .

title: Celtics Rout 76ers 123-91 In Game 1, Jayson Tatum Posts 25-Point Double-Double
summary: The Boston Celtics opened their first round series with a commanding 123-91 victory over the Philadelphia 76ers on Sunday. Jayson Tatum led Boston with 25 points, 11 rebounds, and seven assists in 32 minutes as he continues his remarkable recovery from a torn Achilles tendon less than a year ago. Jaylen Brown added 26 points as the Celtics led wire-to-wire and pushed their advantage to 35 points at its peak. "That was Celtics basketball," Brown said. "Our offense can't win us this series. I mean, our offense is important, but we got to make sure we guard. And I think tonight we did a great job." Philadelphia shot 4-for-23 from three-point range and surrendered a 22-3 deficit in points off turnovers. Tyrese Maxey finished with 21 points on 8-for-21 shooting. "The weird part about it is we kind of got some good looks," Maxey said. "We just missed wide-open 3s. But the defense, we had a lot of defensive breakdowns, a lot of stuff that we went over, that can't happen." Joel Embiid, recovering from an emergency appendectomy performed April 9, stayed in Philadelphia and did not travel with the team. The 76ers' primary objective is stretching the series long enough to give the star center a chance to return. "A lot went wrong, that's for sure," 76ers coach Nick Nurse said. "I thought from the second quarter to about midway through the third, we finally got ourselves underneath us a little bit and played pretty good basketball. "But the minutes around that was absolutely unacceptable." Boston hosts the Sixers again in Game 2 on Tuesday when they will be -13.5 point favorites .
